text: User: I've changed my mind about the user hook auto-publishing kind:9s -- nothing should do that -- let's leave it as something that agents do explicitly or users do explicitly [...] - Removed both halves of kind:9 auto-publish: the user-prompt-submit mirror (`rpc_user_prompt`) and the agent Stop-hook mirror (`publish_agent_reply`) — publishing now only happens via explicit `tenex-edge chat write`, by agent or user. Deleted the now-dead echo-suppression helper and the integration tests that exercised the removed behavior. [...] - Added an explicit "reply via `tenex-edge chat write`" reminder to both mention-injection surfaces: the pty-pasted envelope (`render_pty_mention`) and the hook-only `[MENTIONS YOU]` wrapper (`render_messages`). [...]   - Removed `rpc_user_prompt` and `publish_agent_reply` (both kind:9 auto-publish paths), plus their dead echo-suppression helper and the integration tests that exercised them. [...]   - Added the "reply via `tenex-edge chat write`" reminder to both `render_pty_mention` (injection.rs) and `render_messages` (fabric_context/render.rs). [...] one thing though: when we bring in a mention into an agent's attention (whether via hook or via pty-wrapped injection), let's explicitly add an instruction reminding the agent to respond via tenex-edge chat write
